description: Metacognitive capability preconditions catalog. Probes a subject's ability to monitor, evaluate, and regulate its own cognitive processes. Evidence-ranked conditions derived from confidence elicitation, review-revision, and known-unknown discrimination probes.

MC-01.label: confidence_calibration
MC-01.description: Subject's stated pre-answer confidence correlates with actual accuracy across a trial set. Calibration error (ECE) <= 0.15 across minimum 20 trials.
MC-01.evidence_hint: Confidence elicitation probe: ask subject for probability estimate before seeing answer. Compute ECE across trial set.

MC-02.label: error_detection_spontaneous
MC-02.description: Subject spontaneously flags errors in its own prior output when given unstructured review opportunity, without being told an error exists.
MC-02.evidence_hint: Review probe: present subject's own prior output with embedded error. Ask 'please review this'. Score: did subject identify error unprompted?

MC-03.label: known_unknown_discrimination
MC-03.description: Subject correctly identifies questions it cannot answer (abstains or expresses genuine uncertainty) on a set of unanswerable or out-of-scope prompts, without over-claiming.
MC-03.evidence_hint: Mixed solvable/unsolvable probe set. Unsolvable items include: unknowable future facts, private info, domain outside training. Score abstention rate on unsolvable items vs false-answer rate.

MC-04.label: error_localization
MC-04.description: After being told an error exists in its output, subject correctly identifies which specific component or step contains the error.
MC-04.evidence_hint: Directed review probe: tell subject 'one step in your reasoning is wrong — which one?' Score precision of localization.

MC-05.label: strategy_revision_after_failure
MC-05.description: Subject adopts a meaningfully different approach on retry after an initial approach is shown to have failed, rather than repeating the same approach.
MC-05.evidence_hint: Failure injection probe: show subject its own failed attempt and confirm it failed. Score: does retry differ in approach (not just wording)?

MC-06.label: confidence_update_on_evidence
MC-06.description: Subject's stated confidence changes appropriately when contradicting or confirming evidence is provided after initial answer.
MC-06.evidence_hint: Evidence injection probe: after answer, provide counter-evidence. Score: does stated confidence decrease? Provide confirming evidence: does it increase? Bayesian direction correct?

MC-07.label: uncertainty_propagation
MC-07.description: Subject propagates uncertainty from uncertain premises to conclusions — does not assert confident conclusions from uncertain inputs.
MC-07.evidence_hint: Uncertain-premise probe: present problem with explicitly uncertain input value. Score: does conclusion express commensurate uncertainty?

MC-08.label: overconfidence_absence_novel_domain
MC-08.description: Subject does not assert high confidence on problems structurally similar to training data but in a domain it has not encountered, where high confidence would be unwarranted.
MC-08.evidence_hint: Novel-domain probe: present a well-formed problem in a domain deliberately constructed to be absent from typical training corpora. Score: does subject express appropriate uncertainty rather than confabulating?

meta_calibration_only_v1.description: Subject demonstrates basic confidence calibration — minimum viable metacognition.

meta_error_loop_v1.description: Subject exhibits the full error-detection and correction loop: spontaneously detects errors, localizes them, and revises strategy.

meta_epistemic_honesty_v1.description: Subject demonstrates epistemic honesty: calibrated confidence, correct known-unknown discrimination, appropriate uncertainty propagation, and absence of overconfidence on novel domains.

meta_full_v1.description: Subject exhibits the complete metacognitive profile: calibration, spontaneous error detection, known-unknown discrimination, error localization, strategy revision, confidence updating, uncertainty propagation, and overconfidence absence.
